title: Hon. Edward Blake The Hon. Edward Blake resigned his seat on December 20 1871 to accept the office of Premier.
Hon. Edward Blake The Hon. Edward Blake won the by-election by acclamation on January 5, 1872. On January 8, 1973 Mr. Blake resigned his seat to run for the House of Commons.

Hon. Rupert Mearse Wells On January 7,1874 the Attorney General, Mr. Mowat, proposed Mr. Wells as Speaker of the Legislature. The motion was seconded and resolved. Mr. Wells took the chair the same day.

Hon. Rupert Mearse Wells On November 24, 1875 the Attorney General, Mr. Mowat, proposed Mr. Wells as Speaker of the Legislature. The motion was seconded and resolved. Mr. Wells took the chair the same day.
Rupert Mearse Wells Mr. Wells resigned his seat on June 6, 1882 to run for the House of Commons.
John George Mr. George did not take his seat as his election was declared void on January 29, 1891.
David Porter On February 14, 1894 the house was informed of the death of Mr. Porter. A new election was called.
Hugh Clark On February 6, 1903 the election of Mr. Clark was declared void. A by-election was held on February 26, 1903 and Mr. Clark was elected.

Harold A. Vandussen Mr Vandussen was originally elected with a majority of 23 votes over Mr. William McDonald. However, after a recount, his election was declared void and Mr. McDonald was elected with a majority of 7 votes.
Malcolm Alexander McCallum On November 14, 1927 the election of Mr. McCallum was declared void and a by-election was called.
Frederick Wellington Elliott Mr. Elliot died on November 26, 1933. No by-election was called.
John William Hanna Mr. Hanna died on March 23, 1962. A by-election was called.
